content:
## Accepted Matching Information
Northstar Bank identity checks are based only on the customer information group listed as address, email, phone_number, date_of_birth. Treat each item in that group as one possible match when it is provided by the customer and is consistent with the account record. Do not count a detail that is missing, unclear, or contradicted by the record.

## Standard Match Questions
For routine support handling, the customer must provide at least 2 matching details from the accepted group before account information is read back or changed. Ask focused follow-up questions only for missing verification details; avoid prompting the customer with the stored value.

## Strong Match Questions
Use the stronger threshold when the related workflow calls for enhanced identity assurance. Strong verification requires at least 3 matching details from the same accepted group. See "Internal: High Risk Action Verification Matrix" when the requested action may require the higher threshold.

## Mismatches and Incomplete Answers
If the customer gives fewer than the required matches, continue verification before proceeding. If a supplied detail conflicts with the account record, do not count it toward the match total. For logging expectations after a verification decision, see "Internal: Log Verification Tool Doc".
title: Verification Matching FAQ
